author: nflaig
content: agree if it's part of a stable release but do you mean the new gloas events specifically? I don't think we should freeze the spec there yet, we can do breaking changes across devnets easily without requiring a v2

author: nflaig
content: all apis that are versioned (via fork) have a `version` field in the json response and the `Eth-Consensus-Version` header, there are 16 endpoints that have this pattern, eg. `getBlockV2`, `getProposerLookahead`, `getStateV2`, `produceBlockV3` to name a few

author: .paulharris
content: We’re implementing events on master - so if the new code sends different data it’s going to break fulu clients if things they consume changes shape

author: .paulharris
content: I’ll recheck the pool endpoints but they looked inconsistent

author: nflaig
content: I don't see how a gloas event can break fulu clients, we aren't even emitting those yet besides on devnet-0

author: nflaig
content: but we aren't doing that? or which event specifically do you mean

author: .paulharris
content: I can recheck, not at computer. I thought there were events we were adding to

author: nflaig
content: hmm not sure, if that's the case definitley agree, but also adding fields to an event shouldn't break client either way imo

author: nflaig
content: and `/eth/v1/validator/register_validator`, at least it would be nice to finally consolidate those two apis, was proposed a while ago here https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/issues/435
